summary: Iteration 3 — validated deferred email service (SKIPPED gracefully with empty RESEND_API_KEY), Google Auth session endpoint (401 on invalid), full password-reset flow (request→DB token→confirm→login with new password→reused token=400), Jelly edition badge + per-variant stock, luxury hero image, and full regression. 20/20 backend tests pass. All frontend testids present and functional.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~860 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, products, media, admin, checkout) for maintainability.

In /auth/password-reset/request, `origin` is derived from request headers (referer/origin). If both are missing, the emailed reset URL becomes '/reset-password?token=...' (no host). Emails are currently no-ops (RESEND empty) so no user-facing impact today, but before enabling Resend, fall back to a configured PUBLIC_APP_URL env var.
email_service.send_* are properly wrapped for no-op when RESEND_API_KEY is empty and logged as INFO — verified in supervisor logs.
Google session endpoint correctly returns 401 for invalid session_id (not 500). Full flow with real Emergent session not automated (documented mock).
seed_data_creation: Test users created via /auth/register are cleaned up in-test (TEST_ prefix). password_resets rows for test users cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py verifies emails-deferred, password reset (with DB verification via pymongo), Google session invalid path, Jelly edition+stock, and Stripe checkout regression. Frontend testids confirmed: google-login-btn (on /login and /register), forgot-link, forgot-password-page, forgot-email, forgot-submit, reset-submit, edition-badge, stock-info. Hero image loads at natural width 2400. Google button hardcoded to https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=<origin>/auth/callback in GoogleAuth.jsx.
